title: The Man Who Would be King
desc: Hungry for adventure, two soldiers venture beyond British India's NorthWest Frontier in the hopes of founding their own private kingdom. A version of this story appeared on Suspense.

title: Operation Fleur de Lis
desc: A commando raid into Nazi-occupied France is endangered by the feelings one of the commandos develops for a girl in French resistance - a girl whose loyalties are suspect.

title: The Diamond as Big as the Ritz
desc: A student invites a friend to come to the secret estate of his amoral family whose fortune is based on a huge diamond. The family goes to extreme lengths to protect their privacy.

title: Typhoon
desc: An old tramp steamer in the South Seas faces a triple threat—a shipload of angry migrant workers, an impending typhoon, and a captain who refuses to heed warnings. See also "A Sleeping Draught" (Escape).

title: Sire de Maltroit's Door
desc: A young man is lured into a trap by a noble bent on taking action against him for his advances toward the noble's niece. But does the sire have the right man? A version of this story also appeared on an Australian series, Playhouse of World Famous Authors, and on CBS Radio Mystery Theater.

title: Ring of Thoth
desc: A scholar engaged in research at the Egyptology wing of the British Museum encounters a mysterious stranger who relates a bizarre tale of love, betrayal and a terrible fate that reaches back to a time before the Pyramids.

title: The Fourth Man
desc: Three escaped convicts adrift on a raft in the South Seas are rapidly running out of water and tolerance for each other - but their biggest concern is over the fourth man on the raft, a native islander who is as oblivious of them as he is of thirst.

title: Most Dangerous Game
desc: Classic tale of a world renowned hunter who gets shipwrecked on an island owned by a madman who shares his passion for the hunt, except that he hunts only the most dangerous of game - human beings. Versions of this story also appeared on Suspense, The Chase, and Arch Oboler's Plays.

title: Shipment of Mute Fate
desc: A trapper connives the captain of a passenger ship into allowing the transport of a bushmaster - the world's deadliest snake - with predictable consequences. Two versions of this story were also produced for Suspense.

title: Fall of the House of Usher
desc: Edgar Allen Poe's morbid tale of a man descending into madness over a family curse. Versions of this story also appeared on *CBS Radio Mystery Theater*, *Escape*, *NBC Presents: Short Story*, and *The Weird Circle*.

title: Pollock and the Porroh Man
desc: An English colonist in Africa desecrates a native holy site. Naturally, the local witch doctor hexes him something nasty. A version of this story was done for a series called Dark Ventures.

title: Evening Primrose
desc: A poet decides that he's had it with the real world and will go live secretly in a department store. But he gets a bit of a shock when he finds a small colony of people who have been living there for some time already.

title: The Young Man with the Cream Tarts
desc: Classic story of two thrill seekers in Victorian London get more than they bargained for when they join a secret society and find themselves unwilling members of a suicide club. The first of three tales that make up the story cycle called "The Suicide Club". Versions of this story appeared on *CBS Radio Mystery Theater* and ABC *Mystery Time* as "The Suicide Club".

title: Casting the Ruins
desc: Edward Dunning, Britain's leading authority on Medieval life, writes a scathing review of a crackpot occultist's paper on ancient curses. In turn, he is given 3 months to live. He is not laughing now. See also "This Will Kill You" (CBS Radio Mystery Theater).

title: Country of the Blind
desc: An adventurer discovers a strange valley in remotest South America whose inhabitants have evolved without sight. When he falls in love with a local girl, the locals want to make him blind too. Versions of this story also appeared on Suspense, Favorite Story (as "Strange Valley"), and SIX times on the BBC.

title: Taboo
desc: Peter and Kyra Vaughan meet Shiravieff while vacationing at Zweibergen, a small village in the Carpathian Mountains, during the Summer of 1926. Three local men have vanished during the past week and the townspeople are convinced a werewolf is responsible. Vaughan and Shiravieff take turns acting as bait to lure the werewolf out so the other can kill it.

title: An Occurrence at Owl Creek Bridge
desc: A Confederate saboteur hanged from a bridge by Union troops gets a reprieve when the rope snaps; can he evade the enemy's pursuit and reach home? Versions of this story also appeared on Suspense and CBS Radio Mystery Theater. See also "Present Tense".

title: Back for Christmas
desc: A man's wife drives him too far and has had control of him for too long, so he starts to make plans... A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: Second Class Passenger
desc: A cruise passenger who bemoans the lack of excitement in his life gets more than he wishes for when he gets himself lost in the shadowed alleys of a Mozambique port. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: Second Class Passenger
desc: A cruise passenger who bemoans the lack of excitement in his life gets more than he wishes for when he gets himself lost in the shadowed alleys of a Mozambique port. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: Leiningen Vs. the Ants
desc: An iron-willed South American plantation owner is determined to prevent a plague of army ants from overrunning his land. Versions of this story appeared on Suspense, Mystery in the Air; also as "The Naked Jungle" (based on the movie of the same name) on Lux Radio Theatre.

title: Papa Benjamin
desc: A small-time jazz band leader uses a voodoo song melody to make it to the big times, but can he escape the voodoo curse that comes with it?

title: Three Good Witnesses
desc: A mild-mannered geological surveyor on a train from Istanbul to Cairo helps out a woman and soon finds himself embroiled with wartime spies. Stars Jack Webb.

title: The Vanishing Lady
desc: In a pre-war Paris hotel, an Englishwoman returning home from India with her daughter is struck by a sudden critical illness. The daughter rushes off to fill the doctor's prescription, but upon returning finds her mother gone and the hotel denying she was ever there.

title: Snake Doctor
desc: A wily old hermit in the Ozarks, feared by the locals for his affinity with snakes, incurs the murderous enmity of a backwoods hunter who thinks his wife is having an affair with the 'snake doctor'.

title: Ancient Sorceries
desc: A man stays the night at a remote Welsh village where the residents insist they know him even though he could swear he had never been there before. Starring Paul Frees.

title: How Love Came to Professor Guildea
desc: A minister befriends a psychiatrist who despises all forms of human compassion and attachment, yet becomes convinced some invisible lurking 'thing' is in love with him. Versions of this story also appeared on *CBS Radio Mystery Theater* (as "Shadow of Love"), *Escape* , *Radio City Playhouse*, and *The Vanishing Point*. See also "The Thing in the Hall" (*Theatre 10:30*)

title: Jimmy Goggles, the God
desc: Sailors from a sunken South Seas cargo ship who return to salvage the wreck run afoul of restless natives. Their only saving grace may be their deep sea diving outfit, which the natives take for a god.

title: Misfortune Isle
desc: In the Philippines in 1790, an American merchant decides to try raiding the lair of headhunter pirates that look like easy pickings. However, on his way, just about everything that could go wrong does so. Starring Paul Frees.

title: Shipment of Mute Fate
desc: A trapper connives the captain of a passenger ship into allowing the transport of a bushmaster - the world's deadliest snake - with predictable consequences. Two versions of this story were also produced for Suspense.

title: Action
desc: Following a stroke, a wealthy financier decides to climb an unassailable glacier in the Swiss Alps. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: The Brute
desc: The story of a family with a sailing tradition and their encounter with a beautiful but murderous ship.

title: The Drums of the Fore and Aft
desc: Two incorrigible fife and drum boys serving in the British Army on the Afghan frontier hunger for their first taste of combat, despite the reputation of their regiment for cowardice.

title: The Fourth Man
desc: Three escaped convicts adrift on a raft in the South Seas are rapidly running out of water and tolerance for each other - but their biggest concern is over the fourth man on the raft, a native islander who is as oblivious of them as he is of thirst.

title: John Jack Todd
desc: A longshoreman who takes a job on a river wharf in the steaming jungles of West Africa finds himself the target of a sadistic dock boss.

title: The Time Machine
desc: A classic story of a man who travels into the distant future and discovers a Utopia of idle leisure which hides a terrible secret. Versions of this story also appeared on Favorite Story, the BBC, Seeing Ear Theatre, and Alien Voices.

title: The Match
desc: A Mountie and his prisoner must cross a frozen Canadian wilderness without food or warm clothing when a fire destroys their supplies. But it is the prisoner who has the upper hand, for only he knows the location of a cabin they might reach before they freeze to death.

title: Leiningen Versus the Ants
desc: An iron-willed South American plantation owner is determined to prevent a plague of army ants from overrunning his land. Versions of this story appeared on Suspense, Mystery in the Air; also as "The Naked Jungle" (based on the movie of the same name) on Lux Radio Theatre.

title: Beau Geste
desc: The classic story of three English brothers who join the Foreign Legion for adventure, and instead end up running afoul of a sadistic sergeant and defending a patch of nowhere against Berber hordes.

title: Country of the Blind
desc: An adventurer discovers a strange valley in remotest South America whose inhabitants have evolved without sight. When he falls in love with a local girl, the locals want to make him blind too. Versions of this story also appeared on Suspense, Favorite Story (as "Strange Valley"), and SIX times on the BBC.

title: A Tooth for Paul Revere
desc: A man with a toothache seeks help for his ailment and in so doing ignites the American Revolution.

title: She
desc: A classic story about two explorer's search for a lost city in the jungles of East Africa and their discovery of the secret of immortality.

title: Habit
desc: A sour, crotchety old sea captain, who does not take kindly to snide remarks about his rusty relic of a ship, risks his crew to prove his critics wrong.

title: The Man Who Would be King
desc: Hungry for adventure, two soldiers venture beyond British India's NorthWest Frontier in the hopes of founding their own private kingdom. A version of this story appeared on Suspense.

title: The Fugitive
desc: A French convict escapes his prison and makes his way towards Spain, pursued by the only man he considers his intellectual equal - the chief of police.

title: Ss San Pedro
desc: After taking on an unusual passenger, a ship starts experiencing a bizarre string of inexplicable and escalating troubles. A version of this story was done for Columbia Workshop.

title: Diamond as Big as the Ritz
desc: A student invites a friend to come to the secret estate of his amoral family whose fortune is based on a huge diamond. The family goes to extreme lengths to protect their privacy.

title: Dream of Armegeddon
desc: A man is tormented by persistent dreams of living another life, one in a dark future where he is a political leader unable to prevent the onslaught of global war. Another version appeared on *2000x* (as "A Dream of Armageddon").

title: Evening Primrose
desc: A poet decides that he's had it with the real world and will go live secretly in a department store. But he gets a bit of a shock when he finds a small colony of people who have been living there for some time already.

title: The Man Who Could Work Miracles
desc: George Fotheringay has discovered the secret to performing miracles - all he has to do is ask and it is done. the only thing he can't seem to control are the consequences. Other versions were produced for the CBS Radio Mystery Theater and the BBC

title: The Lost Special
desc: A 40-ton locomotive disappears off the face of the earth on a section of single track between Liverpool and Manchester. Ben Wright plays the Scotland Yard snoop.

title: The Orient Express
desc: A man offers to help out a lady and what happens? He finds himself embroiled in a covert operation to smuggle a freedom fighter out of his native country, of course. See also "The Train from Obisfelde" (Escape).

title: Red Wine
desc: A detective arriving in the jungles of Borneo to investigate a murder narrows down the list of suspects to three. He has a four hour window to figure out which one of them is guilty and take them back to justice. The real question here is who is more clever, the detective or the murderer?

title: Conquerer's Isle
desc: A WWII bomber crew in the Pacific is forced down and find themselves on a remote island populated by an advanced race of mutants planning to take over the world. Another version was produced for *Sleep No More*.

title: He Who Rides the Tiger
desc: A man seeking to save Chinese art treasures from the advancing Japanese army during World War II awakens in a small Chinese village with amnesia years after the war.

title: Shipment of Mute Fate
desc: A trapper connives the captain of a passenger ship into allowing the transport of a bushmaster - the world's deadliest snake - with predictable consequences. Two versions of this story were also produced for Suspense.

title: Finger of Doom
desc: When a young man's fiancee disappears while dropping off a parcel for her boss, he can't convince anyone that she even exists, let alone help him find out what has happened to her. Another version appeared as "I Won't Take A Minute" on Suspense.

title: Country of the Blind
desc: An adventurer discovers a strange valley in remotest South America whose inhabitants have evolved without sight. When he falls in love with a local girl, the locals want to make him blind too. Versions of this story also appeared on Suspense, Favorite Story (as "Strange Valley"), and SIX times on the BBC.

title: Adaptive Ultimate
desc: A life-enhancing serum gives a dying woman incredible powers, and an insatiable lust for power.

title: The Diamond as Big as the Ritz
desc: A student invites a friend to come to the secret estate of his amoral family whose fortune is based on a huge diamond. The family goes to extreme lengths to protect their privacy.

title: When the Man Comes, Follow Him
desc: A mobster's bodyguard narrates the story of a blind gangster who, following the cryptic clues of his dying brother, goes hunting treasure.

title: The General Died at Dawn
desc: A broke American gets involved with embezzling funds from a brutal rebel Chinese general.

title: The Great Impersonation
desc: One the eve of WW II, a Nazi agent assumes the identity of a captured English lord, but runs afoul of not only his doppelganger's past, but his own.

title: The Fourth Man
desc: Three escaped convicts adrift on a raft in the South Seas are rapidly running out of water and tolerance for each other - but their biggest concern is over the fourth man on the raft, a native islander who is as oblivious of them as he is of thirst.

title: The Drums of the Fore and Aft
desc: Two incorrigible fife and drum boys serving in the British Army on the Afghan frontier hunger for their first taste of combat, despite the reputation of their regiment for cowardice.

title: Action
desc: Following a stroke, a wealthy financier decides to climb an unassailable glacier in the Swiss Alps. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: Second Class Passenger
desc: A cruise passenger who bemoans the lack of excitement in his life gets more than he wishes for when he gets himself lost in the shadowed alleys of a Mozambique port. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: Leiningen Versus the Ants
desc: An iron-willed South American plantation owner is determined to prevent a plague of army ants from overrunning his land. Versions of this story appeared on Suspense, Mystery in the Air; also as "The Naked Jungle" (based on the movie of the same name) on Lux Radio Theatre.

title: Red Wine
desc: A detective arriving in the jungles of Borneo to investigate a murder narrows down the list of suspects to three. He has a four hour window to figure out which one of them is guilty and take them back to justice. The real question here is who is more clever, the detective or the murderer?

title: Snake Doctor
desc: A wily old hermit in the Ozarks, feared by the locals for his affinity with snakes, incurs the murderous enmity of a backwoods hunter who thinks his wife is having an affair with the 'snake doctor'.

title: Evening Primrose
desc: A poet decides that he's had it with the real world and will go live secretly in a department store. But he gets a bit of a shock when he finds a small colony of people who have been living there for some time already.

title: Fortune of Vargas
desc: A man learns from a former Mexican revolutionary the location of a stolen gold shipment that was buried in the desert many years ago. It's no big surprise when others take an interest in his little expedition.

title: The Sure Thing
desc: A doctor, responding to a distress call from a sailing yacht in the Caribbean, becomes embroiled in a deadly game of betrayal, greed and murder.

title: Night in Havana
desc: A small time Cuban thug has decided to go straight, but gets involved in one last deal.

title: Flood on the Goodwin
desc: A salvage crew helping to rescue ships in the English Channel during the war inadvertently takes on a Nazi saboteur. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: Plunder of the Sun
desc: A man escorting an old archaeologist who possesses an Incan manuscript detailing the location of a cache of Inca gold ends up in a mess of double-cross, triple-cross, and quadruple-cross. Starring Paul Frees.

title: Three Skeleton Key
desc: Three men are besieged in a lighthouse when a ship wrecks on the shoal and releases a horde of starving rats. Other versions of this story were produced for *Sleep No More* and Suspense (x2). Also, a version was created for an independently produced series called *The Grip of Terror*.

title: Maracas
desc: The first mate of a Caribbean tramp freighter discovers that the ship is carrying a Pandora's Box of rifles, and gets himself entangled with gunrunners, betrayal, murder, and drunken revolutionaries.

title: Letter from Jason
desc: An alcoholic makes a deal with his uncle to kill a man in exchange for future financial support for his family once he commits suicide.

title: Command
desc: A look into the lives of US cavalry troopers on a search and rescue mission into hostile Sioux territory. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense. See also "Massacre at Little Big Horn" (Suspense).

title: Border Town
desc: A down on his luck actor comes across a stash of counterfeit money and decides to go across the border to Mexico to spend it. Starring Jack Webb.

title: Figure a Dame
desc: On a train to Calcutta, the embittered bodyguard of a wealthy widow murders her for the fabulous emerald he thinks will change his life. But... figure a dame.

title: Seeds of Greed
desc: A besotted, ex-Navy drifter is hired by a Chinese treasure hunter to retrieve a cache of pearls from a sunken ship off the coast of Borneo.

title: The Pistol
desc: A frontier tale of a man who sets out to seek revenge on the claim jumpers who murdered his brother.

title: Vanishing Lady
desc: In a pre-war Paris hotel, an Englishwoman returning home from India with her daughter is struck by a sudden critical illness. The daughter rushes off to fill the doctor's prescription, but upon returning finds her mother gone and the hotel denying she was ever there.

title: The Sure Thing
desc: A doctor, responding to a distress call from a sailing yacht in the Caribbean, becomes embroiled in a deadly game of betrayal, greed and murder.

title: Treasure Inc.
desc: A Carribean island serves as the backdrop for an unhappy couple who go into business with a third man and find out that a paradise retreat isn't all that it is cracked up to be. Their plan is to have a resort getaway and seed it with old "treasure" for guests to find, but a resident hermit acts as the proverbial fly in the ointment, hoping to dissuade progress and the onset of civilization.

title: Present Tense
desc: A condemned murderer on his way to death row for killing his wife is granted a reprieve by fate when the train he is on derails and he escapes to find the man who framed him. Stars Vincent Price. Another version of this story (also with Price) appeared on *Suspense*. See also "An Occurrance at Owl Creek Bridge."

title: The Outer Limit
desc: A test pilot in an experimental high altitude aircraft disappears from radar, only to return safely ten hours after he should have run out of fuel. He can't explain, but brings a grim warning back with him. A very popular story among radio producers, versions of this episode also appeared on Dimension X, X Minus One, Beyond Tomorrow, and two versions for Suspense.

title: Two If by Sea
desc: A British journalist must try to arrange the escape from Russia of his Soviet ballerina wife.

title: The Red Mark
desc: Gritty tale of conflict between prisoners and a villainous trustee/executioner over the trustee's intended wife, set on the French penal colony of New Caledonia.

title: The Man Who Won the War
desc: A WWI ship's captain's unsupported claim that he stopped a German invasion must wait many years for the truth behind his fantastic story to finally surface. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: Port Royal
desc: An ex-merchant seaman turned violent hood browbeats his way into a place amongst a diving expedition to the Caribbean to retrieve treasure from a city that had sunk in an earthquake centuries before. Suggested by events in the book *I Dive For Treasure* (1942) by Lt Harry E. Rieseberg.

title: Three Skeleton Key
desc: Three men are besieged in a lighthouse when a ship wrecks on the shoal and releases a horde of starving rats. Other versions of this story were produced for *Sleep No More* and Suspense (x2). Also, a version was created for an independently produced series called *The Grip of Terror*.

title: Danger at Matecumbe
desc: A man desperate for work accepts a job posing as a controversial author.

title: Green Splotches
desc: Ignoring warnings not to enter the Valley of the Infernal River, a scientific expedition encounters a superior alien life form doing some specimen-collecting of their own. Adapted from a T.S. Stribling story. Stars William Conrad, Paul Frees, Ted de Corsia, Jay Novello.

title: Ambassador of Poker
desc: A gentlemanly poker shark from Virginia seeks to swindle a Chinese warlord out of a precious relic.

title: Golden Snake
desc: A down and out pilot is tempted by money and a beautiful woman to fly an archaeologist deep into the Yucatan in search of a sacred Mayan temple, despite native threats, and the disappearance of two previous expeditions.

title: Shanghai Document
desc: Seeking passage from the interior of China to Shanghai, an American journalist manages to get aboard a crowded river steamer, but finds himself drawn into a web of intrigue and violence over the whereabouts of the mysterious Shanghai Document, and the obligatory beautiful woman who seems to be at the center of it all.

title: Something for Nothing
desc: A drifter witnesses a woman leap from a car just before it careens off a cliff, and sees an opportunity to indulge in a little honest blackmail. Bad idea...

title: The Man Who Stole the Bible
desc: A New Orleans hotel guest snatches a bible from his room and finds himself the target of some very desperate and dangerous people. Story by John and Gwen Bagni. Two versions were produced for *Escape* and one version for Suspense.

title: Rim of Terror
desc: A young woman in a Bentley convertible befriends a hitchhiker who's trying to escape his life as a spy and be a regular American. Unfortunately, the sinister "left arm" keeps trying to pull him back. Hans Conreid plays the unwilling spy.

title: Pass to Berlin
desc: After an American soldier stationed in occupied Berlin inadvertently murders a German fraulein, he finds himself the victim of blackmail from a pair of stage show mind-readers.

title: Command
desc: A look into the lives of US cavalry troopers on a search and rescue mission into hostile Sioux territory. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense. See also "Massacre at Little Big Horn" (Suspense).

title: Mars is Heaven
desc: The first explorers to land on Mars discover - Earth, and an idyllic Midwest town populated by their long lost relatives. Story by Ray Bradbury; part of his Martian Chronicles series. Versions of this story also appeared on Dimension X, X Minus One, and ABC Radio Workshop ("Think"). See the full listing of Martian Chronicles stories under Ray Bradbury on the Famous Authors on Radio page.

title: Serenade for a Cobra
desc: A rough 'n ready unemployed pilot in India accepts a job offer to smuggle undesirables out of China, but runs afoul of an Indian manservant who plots his untimely demise. Starring Charles McGraw.

title: Sundown
desc: A farmhand takes exception when a notorious gunslinger commandeers his horse and rides the animal to death. He vows revenge at any cost.

title: Blood Bath
desc: Five men trek deep into the steaming jungles of the Amazon to verify a uranium find that could make them all billionaires - and escalate the nuclear arms race. They each vow to share in the profits, but what sense of morality can you expect from me willing to endanger the human race for their own personal welfare? Starring Vincent Price.

title: Shipment of Mute Fate
desc: A trapper connives the captain of a passenger ship into allowing the transport of a bushmaster - the world's deadliest snake - with predictable consequences. Two versions of this story were also produced for Suspense.

title: Shark Bait
desc: A fishing boat skipper in Nicaragua is hijacked by gunrunners to a deadly rendezvous with rebels from neighbouring Honduras.

title: Yellow Wake
desc: A ruthless treasure hunter murders a whaling ship's first mate to get his hands on the man's treasure map, then usurps his post as part of a plan to seize the vessel and then the treasure buried in the Panamanian jungle.

title: Poison
desc: An American engineer on contract in India has his excessive bigotry tested to the limit when he wakes to find a deadly snake in his bed and must rely on a native doctor to save him. With Jack Webb and William Conrad.

title: Two Came Back
desc: A man gets sucked into a treasure-hunting scheme by an old war buddy whose life he once saved and his buddy's unfaithful vixen of a wife. The hunt takes them to remotest New Guinea, but jungle fever and headhunters soon turn out to be the least of his worries.

title: Red Forest
desc: After tracking down and killing a man for framing him, a murderer flees the remote cabin and inadvertently starts a raging forest fire, which local authorities draft him into helping to quench.

title: The Footprint
desc: An expedition to recover treasure from a remote temple in the Mexican desert runs afoul of a fanatical cult.

title: Crossing Paris
desc: A timid man most smuggle illicit cargo across Nazi occupied Paris with a loudmouthed madman as company.

title: Sleeping Draught
desc: A 19th century prison hulk delivering convicts to the penal colony of Australia is endangered when the desperate prisoners decide to stage a revolt. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense. See also "Typhoon" (Escape).

title: Roulette
desc: A wet-behind-the-ears English college student is seduced by a beautiful woman into taking her to Turkey via train in exchange for $50,000; sounds like easy money, if it weren't for the killers who are after her.

title: Power of Hammer
desc: A thinly disguised take on Joseph Conrad's Heart of Darkness - a safari guide is recruited by Belgian authorities to lead an expedition into the deep Congo to arrest a megalomaniac who has made himself the master of a fanatical native tribe. See also "Eye of Evil" (Escape)

title: Time Machine
desc: A classic story of a man who travels into the distant future and discovers a Utopia of idle leisure which hides a terrible secret. Versions of this story also appeared on Favorite Story, the BBC, Seeing Ear Theatre, and Alien Voices.

title: Seven Hours to Freedom
desc: Two mackerel fishermen are sea-jacked by a group of desperate and murderous criminals.

title: Earth Abides Part 1
desc: After a plague has wiped out the bulk of humanity, a small community of survivors struggle to rebuild civilization, but the same old problems of greed and violence stand in the way. This episode was done in 2 parts.

title: Earth Abides Part 2
desc: After a plague has wiped out the bulk of humanity, a small community of survivors struggle to rebuild civilization, but the same old problems of greed and violence stand in the way. This episode was done in 2 parts.

title: Journey into Fear
desc: A British engineer working for the Turkish government becomes a target for enemy agents and is forced to flee the country on board a ship bound for Athens. However, someone aboard is still out to kill him...

title: Funeral Fires
desc: A hard-nosed doctor sent to battle plague in Malaya must also contend with the theft of his serum and a murder.

title: This Side of Nowhere
desc: A pilot takes an interest in a beautiful woman during a layover, only to have her hijack him to fly her to a remote location in northern Mexico to evade the law.

title: A Passenger to Bali
desc: As the good ship Roundabout readies for departure from Shanghai to Bali, a stranger approaches Captain English and asks for passage. The stranger claims to be a Dutch missionary bringing Bibles and religion to the natives; he presents his passport, character references, and offers a healthy 'fee' to alleviate any misgivings the captain might have. The captain agrees... but once underway soon regrets his decision. Versions were produced for *Escape*, *Mercury Theatre on the Air*, *Mercury Summer Theatre of the Air* and *Sleep No More*.

title: Wild Jack Rett
desc: Red Mesa, a lawless western town, turns to a hired professional killer for peace.

title: The Cave
desc: A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: The Man Who Could Work Miracles
desc: George Fotheringay has discovered the secret to performing miracles - all he has to do is ask and it is done. the only thing he can't seem to control are the consequences. Other versions were produced for the CBS Radio Mystery Theater and the BBC

title: Conquest
desc: A gripping account of a mountain climber's obsession to be the first man to reach the top of Mt. Everest.

title: A Bullet for Mr. Smith
desc: An American Secret Service agent in France undertakes a mission to 'remove' an elusive mystery assassin known only as 'Mr. Smith'.

title: Killer Mine
desc: An army deserter is blackmailed into accepting the dangerous job of blasting open an old mine beneath the sea bed off the coast of Cornwall, to flood it as an underground conduit for a band of smugglers.

title: The Follower
desc: Returning home from abroad, a husband discovers his wife's ex-lover dead on the floor and his wife missing, apparently fled to Mexico.

title: The Island
desc: Okinawa, 1945 - Two American soldiers on patrol behind enemy lines encounter a beautiful girl who tells them of the island she came from, the idyllic paradise one of them has always dreamed of finding and will do anything to reach.

title: Macao
desc: Another version appeared on the CBS series *Romance* as "Red Angel".

title: The Gladiator
desc: A champion heavyweight boxer is kidnapped by a wealthy man with a fixation on ancient Rome . The wealthy man owns an island off the African coast set up to recreate his ideal. Right down to the gladiator arena. Stars William Conrad.

title: Up Periscope
desc: Just prior to WW II, an American ex-submarine captain accepts a commission from the Chinese government to repair an old sub and take it into action against Japanese convoys supplying their war in China. See also "Pressure" (Escape).

title: Rough Shoot
desc: Scaring off what he thinks are poachers from a neighbor's field, a man accidentally kills one and soon finds himself embroiled with former high-ranking Nazis.

title: The Silent Horror
desc: A jilted lover seeks escape and forgetfulness among the primitive tribes of New Guinea, but he must first deal with a local chief who is convinced he has come to overthrow him.

title: The Man Who Stole the Bible
desc: A New Orleans hotel guest snatches a bible from his room and finds himself the target of some very desperate and dangerous people. Story by John and Gwen Bagni. Two versions were produced for *Escape* and one version for Suspense.

title: Gringo
desc: An American boat skipper agrees to help a Mexican adventurer and his gold-digging girlfriend retrieve a fortune from a plane that crashed offshore on the lip of an active underwater volcano. The story was later rewritten slightly for the episode "The Coward".

title: Price of the Head
desc: A violent alcoholic beach bum in the South Seas is continually rescued from dire straits by his native companion - but what possible reason could he have for going to all the trouble?

title: Robert of Huntington
desc: The Sheriff of Nottingham dispatches an assassin into Sherwood Forest to rid himself of the pesky Robin Hood.

title: The Running Man
desc: Lucky in love, unlucky at cards describes the lot of a plantation owner in Honduras, but the tables turn when his wandering eye incurs the jealous wrath of a local voodoo priestess.

title: The Return
desc: A world-weary adventurer longs for what he believes cannot exist : a place without strife or conflict or suffering - until he is clubbed in an alley and wakes to find himself in an island paradise where all his dream come true.

title: Incident in Quito
desc: An archaeologist who in Ecuador seeks to make his reputation by getting rare shrunken heads, and begins the long road... to losing his own.

title: Four Went Home
desc: With the Allied forces closing in, a group of American POWs plot an escape from a German prison camp, fearing their captors may execute them before they can be liberated.

title: The Man Who Liked Dickens
desc: Versions of this story were also produced for Suspense and The Vanishing Point.

title: Conquerer's Isle
desc: A WWII bomber crew in the Pacific is forced down and find themselves on a remote island populated by an advanced race of mutants planning to take over the world. Another version was produced for *Sleep No More*.

title: A Matter of Concience
desc: Dutch officials in colonial Bali are faced with a sudden uprising when a trio of English tourists defile a native temple and steal off with the high priest's daughter.

title: Diary of a Madman
desc: A squad of German soldiers fleeing defeat in North Africa follow a megalomaniacal officer obsessed with carving out a kingdom of their own in the remote interior of the continent. But first they must cross the cruelest place on Earth - the furnace of the Sahara.

title: A Study in Wax
desc: Two men trapped for the winter at a remote research station in the Arctic Circle must contend against each other as well as the elements. Another version of this story also appeared on Suspense. See also "Weather Station Four" (Nightfall).

title: Jetsam
desc: An alien disregards protocols and contacts

title: Wild Jack Rett
desc: Red Mesa, a lawless western town, turns to a hired professional killer for peace.

title: I Saw Myself Running
desc: Susan falls asleep and meets a woman trapped in her nightmarish dream world. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: The Tramp
desc: A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: The Island
desc: Okinawa, 1945 - Two American soldiers on patrol behind enemy lines encounter a beautiful girl who tells them of the island she came from, the idyllic paradise one of them has always dreamed of finding and will do anything to reach.

title: The Man with the Steel Teeth
desc: A correspondent in Moscow imprisoned and accused of espionage escapes from a Soviet prison and flees his pursuers toward the Iron Curtain. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: Pressure
desc: A hard luck, unpopular sub captain seeks to redeem his reputation by taking on a Japanese aircraft carrier and its escort of destroyers single-handed.

title: The Invader
desc: A milquetoast husband hounded by his wife for his obsession with his ham radio hobby, makes contact with an alien spacecraft on its way to Earth. Very similar to "Pictures Don't Lie" on X Minus One.

title: A Sleeping Draught
desc: A 19th century prison hulk delivering convicts to the penal colony of Australia is endangered when the desperate prisoners decide to stage a revolt. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense. See also "Typhoon" (Escape).

title: Classified Secret
desc: A spy traveling cross-country by bus exchanges military and government secrets with foreign agents. A version of this story was produced for Suspense.

title: El Guitarero
desc: Partial episode directed by Anthony Ellis and written by E. Jack Newman about a love hate triangle in Mexico. Interrupted by prisoner exchange info/news break in. The dialogue has the soft, sweltering heat of Mexican days, and the languid beauty... of Mexican nights. As a guitareo plays a game of life and death... with a poet.

title: The Derelick
desc: The survivors of a sunken ship happen across a drifting derelict, but when they discover gold aboard, they find a far greater menace than being adrift at sea.

title: Lili and the Colonel
desc: A young British Officer stationed in East Africa is befriended by a bigoted, high-handed retired colonel who becomes the target of insurgent Mau-Maus. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: The Vessel of Wrath
desc: On a remote South Seas island, a besotted miscreant and womanizer meets his match in the prudish stalwart daughter of the local pastor.

title: North of Polaris
desc: The first expedition to another planet discovers a world in ruins, where the dominant surviving life form are insatiable hordes of giant hungry rats.

title: The Voyages of Sinbad
desc: Sinbad the sailor relates one of his adventures from his youth, when he took a wife in a distant land, and fell victim to a unique form of native barbarism when she dies of a sudden illness.

title: Clear for Action
desc: A swashbuckler a la Hornblower - an early 19th century naval frigate is ordered into the Caribbean to deal with a French privateer preying on American merchantmen.

title: The Far-away Island
desc: Adam Hamish is living a leisurely life in self-imposed exile when he learns that his past is catching up to him, in the form of an unidentified pursuer. He quickly decides to move on to a “faraway island”, abandoning his traveling companion on the journey. His pursuer proves to be well known to him and both their lives are changed profoundly after they reunite in the village of Omoka. One abandons “civilization” forever, the other returns to embrace it, with all its complications and consequences. -- plot summary by Powel Crosley

title: A Source of Irritation
desc: A crusty English farmer is abducted by a German flyer, who is under the impression he is one of their agents, and taken to Nazi Germany. Later rescued by the British, he is then sentenced to death for spying on behalf of the Nazis.

title: The Out Station
desc: A remote outpost in British Malaya is the crucible for the only two Englishmen assigned there, both of whom hate one another with every fiber of their being.

title: The Open Boat
desc: A straight-up survival tale of four men in a small dory lost in an ocean which is not identified.

title: The Notebook
desc: Two prospectors in the Old West run afoul of a desperate and murderous claim jumper.

title: The Red Forest
desc: After tracking down and killing a man for framing him, a murderer flees the remote cabin and inadvertently starts a raging forest fire, which local authorities draft him into helping to quench.

title: Three Skeleton Key
desc: Three men are besieged in a lighthouse when a ship wrecks on the shoal and releases a horde of starving rats. Other versions of this story were produced for *Sleep No More* and Suspense (x2). Also, a version was created for an independently produced series called *The Grip of Terror*.

title: The Thirteenth Truck
desc: WW II desert commandos on a mission to locate, penetrate and destroy a secret German airfield in North Africa. See also "Operation Fleur-de-Lys" (Escape).

title: The Man from Tomorrow
desc: A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: The Game
desc: A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: Train from Olbiefelde
desc: An spy assigned to escort a defecting nuclear scientist on a train across the Iron Curtain discovers that there is an enemy agent aboard carrying a bomb with which he will blow up the train should it reach its destination. See also "The Orient Express" (Escape).

title: The Abominable Snowman
desc: An expedition into the Himalayas to search for the legendary Yeti finds more than it bargained for. Stars William Conrad.

title: The Log
desc: Upon hearing that his old boat is to be sold for scrap, an ex- submarine captain hires his old crew and steals the ship from under the noses of its owners, intending to lead a life of modern-day piracy on the high seas.

title: The Untouchable
desc: Two men on a business trip to India find their venture disrupted when one, a germophobe, becomes convinced he has contracted leprosy from a street beggar.

title: Zero Hour
desc: Neighborhood children invent a new game called 'Invasion' - or is it just a game? Versions of this story also appeared on Future Tense, Adventure Theater, Suspense (3 versions), X Minus One, The Mysterious Traveler, and Dimension X. !dividing line Copyright © 2000-2025 www.otrplotspot.com

title: Elementals
desc: Eager to prove his theory that love is an illusion, a man offers a couple a small fortune if they agree to his trial by ordeal. Another version of this story was produced for the series Author's Playhouse.

title: Bird of Paradise
desc: A fortune seeker after a source of rare bird skins in the South Seas instead stumbles on a remote island whose only inhabitants are a beautiful girl and her rather violent boyfriend, who is as jealous of his privacy as he is of her.

title: Violent Night
desc: An American plantation owner in a banana republic becomes the prey in a deadly manhunt when a petty thug he once humiliated resurfaces as the leader of a successful revolution and now seeks revenge.

title: The Second Shot
desc: Lieutenant Daumier, a young officer in Napoleon's Grande Armee sports an obsession with fighting pistol duels, which brings him into mortal conflict with an experienced senior officer—who just happens to be engaged to Daumier's former lover. See also "The Shot" by Alexander Pushkin.

title: The Return
desc: A world-weary adventurer longs for what he believes cannot exist : a place without strife or conflict or suffering - until he is clubbed in an alley and wakes to find himself in an island paradise where all his dream come true.

title: The Scarlet Plague
desc: An inexplicable and lightning-quick plague sweeps across the world, killing its victims within an hour of the first symptoms. Civilization rapidly begins to disintegrate. See also "Earth Abides" (Escape).

title: Affair at Mandrake
desc: A British officer is assigned to Mandrake to head military research on rockets; but a German POW has escaped from a nearby prison camp and may have an idea of what's going on at Mandrake.

title: The Adversary
desc: An island haven for crooks, swindlers, smugglers and other nogoodniks receives a visit from 'Honest John Weatherby' , a merchant captain who is eager to demonstrate just how honest a fellow he really is. Maybe a little too eager...

title: Benchillina and the Fisherman
desc: A fishing trip to the back country of Ireland leads a vacationer into a dangerous love triangle wisha beautiful Gypsy girl.

title: Blood Waters
desc: A diver becomes enmeshed with a gang of treasure hunters in the Amazon.

title: Judgement Day at Crippled Deer
desc: The Klondike is the setting for this tale of murder and revenge when a close knit group of friends seek frontier justice against the killer of a missing companion.

title: The Dark Wall
desc: A man must take his schizophrenic wife across the high Pyrenees to get her to the hospital, but her delusions persuade the locals on the way that he is a threat to her life.

title: The Birds
desc: A story of the day when all the birds in the world decided the planet would be better off without humans in it. Adapted by Alfred Hitchcock for a film of the same name. A version of this story was also produced for the Lux Radio Theatre.

title: Eye of Evil
desc: A story with similarities to Joseph Conrad's Heart of Darkness. A representative of a historical research society is sent into the depths of the Burmese jungle to locate a fellow researcher who has seemingly gone mad. However, someone seems to have put the 'evil eye' on his expedition. See also "The Power of Hammer" (Escape)

title: Flood on the Goodwin
desc: A salvage crew helping to rescue ships in the English Channel during the war inadvertently takes on a Nazi saboteur. A version of this story was also produced for Suspense.

title: Night of the Guns
desc: Two American smugglers in Haiti are confused by the natives' demands for their merchandise.

title: The Price of the Head
desc: A violent alcoholic beach bum in the South Seas is continually rescued from dire straits by his native companion - but what possible reason could he have for going to all the trouble?

title: The Coward
desc: The lone survivor of a plane crash in the Caribbean seeks to return to the crash site to retrieve a fortune. VERY similar to the episode "Gringo".

title: Two and Two Make Four
desc: A merchant sailor recovering from a head wound in Calais suffers a blackout after which he finds himself in England and the target of an unknown enemy out to kill him.

title: The King of Owanatu
desc: Two fortune hunters after black pearls find a mother lode on a remote Pacific island, one ruled over by a hermitical tyrant with an arsenal and an army of natives to back up his power.

title: The Boiling Sea
desc: A ship becomes trapped when a volcano rises out of the ocean around them, encircling them in a ring of rock and burning magma.

title: The Target
desc: An insurance investigator on the trail of a con man in Chile gets caught in his target's web of betrayal, deceit and murder.
